name: Carrollton Bank
description_short: Carrollton Bank is an FDIC-insured regional bank offering personal and business banking services across 12 locations in Illinois and Missouri, with free checking, online banking, and lending products.
description_long: Carrollton Bank is a regional banking institution that operates as a traditional community bank with FDIC insurance backing, meaning customer deposits are protected by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank has established a physical presence across Illinois and Missouri with branches in Alton, Arnold, Carrollton, Clayton, Des Peres, Edwardsville, Jerseyville, O'Fallon, St. Charles, St. Louis Hills, and Springfield, plus a headquarters location. This footprint suggests a mid-sized regional bank focused on serving both individuals and small-to-medium businesses in the Midwest. Carrollton Bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including free checking accounts, free ATM access, online and mobile banking platforms, credit cards, home loans, home equity loans, money market accounts, and savings products (HSAs and CDs). On the business side, they provide small business and commercial banking with dedicated business bankers, business online and mobile banking, business credit cards, business money market accounts, and loan payment capabilities. The bank also emphasizes fraud prevention measures and offers financial education resources to customers. What distinguishes Carrollton Bank is its positioning as a traditional, FDIC-insured community bank with physical branch locations and named staff members (such as Commercial Banking officer Dan Abegg), suggesting a relationship-focused approach rather than a purely digital-first model. They actively market free checking and ATM access, indicating competitive emphasis on accessible banking. The bank maintains both personal and business banking divisions with specialized services, demonstrating breadth across customer segments. As a regional bank, Carrollton Bank is best suited for customers in their service areas seeking traditional banking relationships with physical branch access. Customers should verify current rates, fees, and product availability through direct contact or branch visits. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the U.S. Government
Free checking accounts available for personal customers
Free ATM access across their network
12 physical branch locations in Illinois and Missouri for in-person service
Dedicated business bankers for commercial and small business clients
Online and mobile banking available for both personal and business accounts
Comprehensive lending products including home loans and home equity loans
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Illinois and Missouri
Website lacks specific information about interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules
No details provided about minimum balance requirements or account features
Limited transparency about business loan terms or commercial banking products
No mention of national reach or options for customers outside service areas
best_for: Individuals and families in Illinois and Missouri seeking traditional banking with branch access
Small business owners needing personal relationship-based commercial banking
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and local community bank relationships
Businesses requiring dedicated business banker support and commercial credit products
diagnosis: Carrollton Bank is best for Midwest customers in their service areas seeking traditional FDIC-insured banking with physical branches and personal service. The main caveat is that detailed product information, rates, and fees are not available on their website, requiring direct contact with the bank for specific terms and competitive comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Carrollton Bank is FDIC-insured, offering personal and business banking across 12 locations in Illinois and Missouri with free checking and lending.
